ogTitle: Far Meadow vs Fargunyah: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Far Meadow and Fargunyah.
heading: Far Meadow vs Fargunyah — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Far Meadow, New South Wales and Fargunyah, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Fargunyah has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Far Meadow (45/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Far Meadow or Fargunyah safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Far Meadow has a flood risk score of 41/100, while Fargunyah scores 55/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Far Meadow scores 64/100 for bushfire risk. Fargunyah scores 31/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Far Meadow or Fargunyah?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Fargunyah has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.23% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
